title: spergularia modesta proles urbica
intro: Spergularia modesta proles urbica, also known as the urban sandwort or weed spurry, is a plant species in the carnation family Caryophyllaceae. It is a low-growing, annual herb native to Europe and Western Asia, often found in sandy soils and disturbed areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Spergularia modesta proles urbica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
